what: File-first architecture saved us during every bug fix
why_it_worked: JSON files on disk = grep + bulk-edit + git diff. Fixed 37 bugs by editing files directly. Would have been 10x harder in a DB.
repeat_when: Always keep files as source of truth for knowledge systems

what: Autonomous daemon extracted 4315 obs across 42 accounts
why_it_worked: 30-min cycles, per-account isolation (one crash doesn't kill the batch), auto-commit + Drive sync + GitHub push. Ran 358 cycles without human intervention.
repeat_when: For any large extraction/enrichment job, build an autonomous daemon with per-item isolation and auto-commit

what: Pre-commit hook caught 38 non-canonical sector values
why_it_worked: guard_data_quality.py found beauty/retail obs on first run after adding sector alias validation. Would have been a silent data quality issue.
repeat_when: Add validation for new rules IMMEDIATELY and run against existing data — the first run always catches something

what: Round-robin queue fixed sector bias in analytics
why_it_worked: Reordering from sequential (26 f_and_b first) to round-robin by sector immediately balanced the analytics output.
repeat_when: When processing data for analytics, always randomize or round-robin — sequential order creates invisible bias

what: validate_all.py as gatekeeper prevented bad data from entering the system
why_it_worked: 4315/4315 PASS on every commit. The daemon rolls back any commit that fails validation. Zero bad records in the database.
repeat_when: Always have a schema validation gate before any commit/sync. Make it impossible to persist invalid data.

what: Built 10-endpoint intelligence API in one session
why_it_worked: Clear data model (Postgres with known schema) + FastAPI boilerplate speed + existing analytics data to query. Having the data ready made the API trivial.
repeat_when: Always get data quality right BEFORE building the API layer

what: Demo page made the API usable by humans
why_it_worked: Single HTML file with fetch() calls. No build step, no framework. Served from FastAPI static. Took 30 minutes.
repeat_when: For internal tools, a single HTML file beats a React app. Ship the simplest thing that works.

what: Pre-build questions prevented bugs during this session
why_it_worked: The 5 questions checklist caught the provenance schema issue (last_verified_at not in schema) before it became a real problem. The guard script found 38 non-canonical sectors on first run.
repeat_when: Always run verification scripts on new changes immediately — the first run catches something.
